title: RHEL system tests: remote (root and rootless) `not ok 203 [272] podman system connection - tcp`
body:
### Issue Description

```
not ok 203 [272] podman system connection - tcp in 6092ms
# (from function `bail-now' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 187,
#  from function `die' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 980,
#  from function `wait_for_port' in file test/system/helpers.network.bash, line 440,
#  in test file test/system/272-system-connection.bats, line 115)
#   `wait_for_port 127.0.0.1 $_SERVICE_PORT' failed
#
# [12:52:05.300869422] $ /var/ARTIFACTS/work-rootless-remotejn317ebo/plans/system/rootless-remote/tree/bin/podman-remote system connection add myconnect tcp://localhost:64985
#
# [12:52:05.332736648] $ /var/ARTIFACTS/work-rootless-remotejn317ebo/plans/system/rootless-remote/tree/bin/podman-remote info
# [12:52:05.959494570] OS: linux/amd64
# provider: qemu
# version: 5.4.0
#
# Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
# Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: Get "http://localhost:64985/v5.4.0/libpod/_ping": dial tcp [::1]:64985: connect: connection refused
# [12:52:05.963381579] [ rc=125 (expected) ]
# /var/ARTIFACTS/work-rootless-remotejn317ebo/plans/system/rootless-remote/tree/test/system/272-system-connection.bats: line 111: /var/ARTIFACTS/work-rootless: No such file or directory
# #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
# #| FAIL: Timed out waiting for 127.0.0.1:64985
# #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
#
# [12:52:11.120907802] $ /var/ARTIFACTS/work-rootless-remotejn317ebo/plans/system/rootless-remote/tree/bin/podman-remote system connection rm --all
# # [teardown]
```

comments.body: Your testpath/name conflicts with the `${PODMAN%%-remote*}` line as the substation no longer works correctly.

> Your testpath/name conflicts with the `${PODMAN%%-remote*}` line as the substation no longer works correctly.

is it not already handled in `make remotesystem`? I am running the makefile targets directly in the TMT jobs without any additional envvar modification as there are no rpms being built in the RHEL PR anymore. If it's not yet handled, I can probably look at changing that.

```
$ PODMAN=/var/ARTIFACTS/work-rootless-remotejn317ebo/plans/system/rootless-remote/tree/bin/podman-remote
$ echo ${PODMAN%%-remote*}
/var/ARTIFACTS/work-rootless
```

That is wrong because the path tmt uses contain --remote in its name. Of course the substitution being incorrect is the real issue so that should likely be fixed instead but this means fixing it on main and then backporting into the RHEL branch.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
